metadata.scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Pichhda Varg Evam Ati Pichhda Varg Kaushal Vikas Prashikshan Yojana

raw_data.details: The scheme "Mukhyamantri Pichhda Varg Evam Ati Pichhda Varg Kaushal Vikas Prashikshan Yojana" was launched by the Backward Classes and Extremely Backward Classes Welfare Department, Government of Bihar. It aims to enhance the employability and income-generating capacity of youth from backward and extremely backward communities through skill development training. Under this scheme, free domain-specific skilling programs are provided to youth to help them become job-ready or self-employed. Scheme ImplementationThe scheme is implemented by the Bihar State Backward Classes Finance and Development Corporation, in coordination with the Bihar Skill Development Mission (BSDM). TrainingTraining is conducted through Skill Development Centres (SDCs) approved by BSDM under various sectors like IT, construction, beauty & wellness, and more.
raw_data.benefits: Free Skill Training: Domain Skilling Programs in various sectors like Apparel, Beauty & Wellness, Construction, Electronics, IT-ITES, Retail, etc. Certification: Candidates receive certificates post successful completion of training and assessment. Placement Support: Support for employment or self-employment opportunities after course completion.
raw_data.eligibility: The applicant should be a resident of Bihar. The applicant should belong to the Backward Class or Extremely Backward Class category. The applicant should be between 18 and 45 years of age. The applicant should possess the Aadhaar number (mandatory for Aadhaar-based registration).
raw_data.application_process: OfflineApplication Process for the ApplicantVisit the Official PortalInterested candidates must visit the official website of the [Bihar Skill Development Mission](https://skillmissionbihar.org/) (BSDM). Search for Nearest Skill Development Centre (SDC)On the homepage, click on “Click here for your nearest center”. Then select “Kushal Yuva Program - Find Centers”. Choose the KYP course, select a preferred search mode (e.g., by district, block, or pincode), and fill in the required details. A list of Skill Development Centres (SDCs) matching the criteria will be displayed. Document PreparationThe applicant must gather self-attested copies of all necessary documents (Aadhaar, caste certificate, residence proof, educational qualifications, etc.) as per the course requirements. Centre Visit and RegistrationThe applicant should personally visit the nearest Skill Development Centre (SDC) for document verification and registration assistance. Application Process for the Skill Development CentresCandidate IdentificationLocal SDCs identify eligible candidates from the Backward Class (BC) and Extremely Backward Class (EBC) categories through community outreach and awareness activities. Document Collection and UploadAll required documents are collected from the candidate and uploaded to the BSDM portal by the SDC for record and processing. Aadhaar VerificationThe applicant's Aadhaar number is verified digitally to confirm identity and eligibility. Batch FormationA training batch is formed comprising a minimum of 20 and a maximum of 40 candidates. Approval by the CorporationUpon successful formation and verification of the batch, the Bihar State Backward Classes Finance and Development Corporation grants formal approval for the commencement of training. Training DeliveryCertified trainers at BSDM-approved SDCs deliver domain-specific skill training under the prescribed curriculum. Attendance and performance are monitored digitally.
raw_data.documents_required: Aadhaar CardIncome CertificateResidential CertificateCaste CertificateAny Other Document if Required

To know more please visit Check EligibilityMukhyamantri Pichhda Varg Evam Ati Pichhda Varg Kaushal Vikas Prashikshan YojanaBiharMukhyamantri Pichhda Varg Evam Ati Pichhda Varg Kaushal Vikas Prashikshan YojanaBackward ClassesEmploymentExtremely Backward ClassesSkill DevelopmentTrainingCheck EligibilityDetailsThe scheme "Mukhyamantri Pichhda Varg Evam Ati Pichhda Varg Kaushal Vikas Prashikshan Yojana" was launched by the Backward Classes and Extremely Backward Classes Welfare Department, Government of Bihar. It aims to enhance the employability and income-generating capacity of youth from backward and extremely backward communities through skill development training. Under this scheme, free domain-specific skilling programs are provided to youth to help them become job-ready or self-employed. Scheme ImplementationThe scheme is implemented by the Bihar State Backward Classes Finance and Development Corporation, in coordination with the Bihar Skill Development Mission (BSDM). TrainingTraining is conducted through Skill Development Centres (SDCs) approved by BSDM under various sectors like IT, construction, beauty & wellness, and more. BenefitsFree Skill Training: Domain Skilling Programs in various sectors like Apparel, Beauty & Wellness, Construction, Electronics, IT-ITES, Retail, etc. Certification: Candidates receive certificates post successful completion of training and assessment. Placement Support: Support for employment or self-employment opportunities after course completion. EligibilityThe applicant should be a resident of Bihar. The applicant should belong to the Backward Class or Extremely Backward Class category. The applicant should be between 18 and 45 years of age. The applicant should possess the Aadhaar number (mandatory for Aadhaar-based registration). Application ProcessOfflineApplication Process for the ApplicantVisit the Official PortalInterested candidates must visit the official website of the [Bihar Skill Development Mission](https://skillmissionbihar.org/) (BSDM). Search for Nearest Skill Development Centre (SDC)On the homepage, click on “Click here for your nearest center”. Then select “Kushal Yuva Program - Find Centers”. Choose the KYP course, select a preferred search mode (e.g., by district, block, or pincode), and fill in the required details. A list of Skill Development Centres (SDCs) matching the criteria will be displayed. Document PreparationThe applicant must gather self-attested copies of all necessary documents (Aadhaar, caste certificate, residence proof, educational qualifications, etc.) as per the course requirements. Centre Visit and RegistrationThe applicant should personally visit the nearest Skill Development Centre (SDC) for document verification and registration assistance. Application Process for the Skill Development CentresCandidate IdentificationLocal SDCs identify eligible candidates from the Backward Class (BC) and Extremely Backward Class (EBC) categories through community outreach and awareness activities. Document Collection and UploadAll required documents are collected from the candidate and uploaded to the BSDM portal by the SDC for record and processing. Aadhaar VerificationThe applicant's Aadhaar number is verified digitally to confirm identity and eligibility. Batch FormationA training batch is formed comprising a minimum of 20 and a maximum of 40 candidates. Approval by the CorporationUpon successful formation and verification of the batch, the Bihar State Backward Classes Finance and Development Corporation grants formal approval for the commencement of training. Training DeliveryCertified trainers at BSDM-approved SDCs deliver domain-specific skill training under the prescribed curriculum. Attendance and performance are monitored digitally. Documents RequiredAadhaar CardIncome CertificateResidential CertificateCaste CertificateAny Other Document if RequiredFrequently Asked QuestionsWho can apply for this scheme? Residents of Bihar belonging to Backward or Extremely Backward Classes, aged 18-45 years, can apply. Is there any fee for the training? No, the training is completely free under this scheme. What types of training are provided? Training is offered in sectors like Apparel, Beauty & Wellness, Construction, Electronics, IT-ITES, Retail, etc. Is Aadhaar mandatory for application? Yes, Aadhaar-based registration is mandatory. Where can I apply for training? The applicant can approach your nearest BSDM-approved Skill Development Centre. How are candidates selected? Selection is done by SDCs as per eligibility and training requirements set by BSDM. What is the batch size for training? Each batch includes a minimum of 20 and a maximum of 40 candidates. Is there any age relaxation for women or special categories? No such relaxation is specified in the current scheme guidelines. What is the role of the BSDM Portal? It is used for registration, batch approval, attendance tracking, assessment, and placement records. Whom should I contact for more information? The applicant can contact the Bihar State Backward Classes Finance and Development Corporation or your nearest Skill Development Centre. raw_data.scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Pichhda Varg Evam Ati Pichhda Varg Kaushal Vikas Prashikshan Yojana

faqs.question: Who can apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Residents of Bihar belonging to Backward or Extremely Backward Classes, aged 18-45 years, can apply.

faqs.question: Is there any fee for the training?
faqs.answer: No, the training is completely free under this scheme.

faqs.question: What types of training are provided?
faqs.answer: Training is offered in sectors like Apparel, Beauty & Wellness, Construction, Electronics, IT-ITES, Retail, etc.

faqs.question: Where can I apply for training?
faqs.answer: The applicant can approach your nearest BSDM-approved Skill Development Centre.

faqs.question: How are candidates selected?
faqs.answer: Selection is done by SDCs as per eligibility and training requirements set by BSDM.

faqs.question: What is the batch size for training?
faqs.answer: Each batch includes a minimum of 20 and a maximum of 40 candidates.

faqs.question: Is there any age relaxation for women or special categories?
faqs.answer: No such relaxation is specified in the current scheme guidelines.

faqs.question: What is the role of the BSDM Portal?
faqs.answer: It is used for registration, batch approval, attendance tracking, assessment, and placement records.

faqs.question: Whom should I contact for more information?
faqs.answer: The applicant can contact the Bihar State Backward Classes Finance and Development Corporation or your nearest Skill Development Centre.
